title: Pyrus keszthelyensis
intro: Pyrus keszthelyensis, also known as the Hungarian mountain pear, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus keszthelyensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus keszthelyensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Pyrus keszthelyensis is classified as a species of plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Pyrus, which includes various species of pear trees. More specifically, Pyrus keszthelyensis is a member of the subgenus Pyrus, which consists of true pears. This species is native to Hungary and is considered to be a rare and endangered species. Pyrus keszthelyensis is also known as the Hungarian pear or Keszthely pear.
